context.pr_title: Fix incorrect multipler for limit field
context.pr_body:
I want to merge this change because we recently introduced the `values` field with input limit. By accident the query cost was added to incorrect place. This PR fixes this. 

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> The changes on `SelectedAttribute` is expected. We don't have `limit` there, and it is not paginated.  (The query cost for this field will be handled separatly)

<!-- Please mention all relevant issue numbers. -->
<!-- GitHub issue number is required for external contributions. -->

# Impact

- [ ] New migrations
- [ ] New/Updated API fields or mutations
- [ ] Deprecated API fields or mutations
- [ ] Removed API types, fields, or mutations

# Docs

<!-- Docs are stored in a separate repository: https://github.com/saleor/saleor-docs/. -->
<!-- Please provide a link to the PR that updates documentation for your changes. -->
<!-- If changes in docs are not required, please mention that in the description. -->

- [ ] Link to documentation:

# Pull Request Checklist

<!-- Please keep this section. It will make the maintainer's life easier. -->

- [ ] Privileged queries and mutations are either absent or guarded by proper permission checks
- [ ] Database queries are optimized and the number of queries is constant
- [ ] Database migrations are either absent or optimized for zero downtime
- [ ] The changes are covered by test cases
- [ ] All new fields/inputs/mutations have proper labels added (`ADDED_IN_X`, `PREVIEW_FEATURE`, etc.)
- [ ] All migrations have proper dependencies
- [ ] All indexes are added concurrently in migrations
- [ ] All RunSql and RunPython migrations have revert option defined

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README
Commerce that works with your language and stack
GraphQL native, API-only platform for scalable composable commerce.

Get to know Saleor:
Talk to a human | Talk to the API

Join our community:
Website | Twitter | Discord
Blog | Subscribe to newsletter

[![Discord Badge](https://dcbadge.vercel.app/api/server/unUfh24R6d)](https://saleor.io/discord)
## Table of Contents - [What makes Saleor special?](#what-makes-saleor-special) - [Why API-only Architecture?](#why-api-only-architecture) - [Features](#features) - [Installation](#installation) - [Documentation](#documentation) - [Saleor Platform](#saleor-platform) - [Storefront](#storefront) - [Dashboard](#dashboard) - [Contributing](#contributing) - [License](#license) ## What makes Saleor special? - **Technology-agnostic** - no monolithic plugin architecture or technology lock-in. - **GraphQL only** - Not afterthought API design or fragmentation across different styles of API. - **Headless and API only** - APIs are the only way to interact, configure, or extend the backend. - **Open source** - a single version of Saleor without feature fragmentation or commercial limitations. - **Cloud native** - battle tested on global brands. - **Native-multichannel* ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods
agents.input_prompt:
Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
Fix incorrect multipler for limit field

## Description
I want to merge this change because we recently introduced the `values` field with input limit. By accident the query cost was added to incorrect place. This PR fixes this. 

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> The changes on `SelectedAttribute` is expected. We don't have `limit` there, and it is not paginated.  (The query cost for this field will be handled separatly)

<!-- Please mention all relevant issue numbers. -->
<!-- GitHub issue number is required for external contributions. -->

# Impact

- [ ] New migrations
- [ ] New/Updated API fields or mutations
- [ ] Deprecated API fields or mutations
- [ ] Removed API types, fields, or mutations

# Docs

<!-- Docs are stored in a separate repository: https://github.com/saleor/saleor-docs/. -->
<!-- Please provide a link to the PR that updates documentation for your changes. -->
<!-- If changes in docs are not required, please mention that in the description. -->

- [ ] Link to documentation:

# Pull Request Checklist

<!-- Please keep this section. It will make the maintainer's life easier. -->

- [ ] Privileged queries and mutations are either absent or guarded by proper permission checks
- [ ] Database queries are optimized and the number of queries is constant
- [ ] Database migrations are either absent or optimized for zero downtime
- [ ] The changes are covered by test cases
- [ ] All new fields/inputs/mutations have proper labels added (`ADDED_IN_X`, `PREVIEW_FEATURE`, etc.)
- [ ] All migrations have proper dependencies
- [ ] All indexes are added concurrently in migrations
- [ ] All RunSql and RunPython migrations have revert option defined


Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

output.pr_title: Fix incorrect multipler for limit field
output.pr_body:
I want to merge this change because we recently introduced the `values` field with input limit. By accident the query cost was added to incorrect place. This PR fixes this. 

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> The changes on `SelectedAttribute` is expected. We don't have `limit` there, and it is not paginated.  (The query cost for this field will be handled separatly)

Impact checklist: no migrations/api changes/etc marked.
output.masca_optimized:
### MASCA (optimized for this PR)

#### M — Modify (what/where to change)
The bug is in the GraphQL query cost configuration for the newly introduced `Attribute.values(limit: Int)` field. The query-cost “multiplier” should apply to `Attribute.values` (because `limit` argument exists there), but it was mistakenly added under `SelectedAttribute.values` (which has no `limit` argument and is not paginated).

Primary change:
- Move/remove the `limit` multiplier from `SelectedAttribute.values` entry in the cost map.
- Ensure `Attribute.values` entry in the cost map uses `multipliers: ["limit"]`.

#### A — Affected behavior (expected result)
- Query cost should scale with `Attribute.values(limit: ...)`.
- `SelectedAttribute.values` should *not* use `limit` as multiplier (since the argument doesn’t exist there). Its cost should remain constant for now (as noted in the PR description).

#### S — Scope (blast radius)
- Only impacts query cost validation (complexity limiting) and does not change schema, resolvers, or DB.
- Can affect which queries are rejected/accepted under `GRAPHQL_QUERY_MAX_COMPLEXITY`.

#### C — Coverage (tests to add/adjust)
Add/adjust unit tests around `validate_query_cost` to assert:
- Cost increases when querying `Attribute.values(limit: N)`.
- No cost multiplier is applied for `SelectedAttribute.values`.

Most suitable place is the existing cost validator tests.

#### A — Acceptance criteria (done when)
- `saleor/graphql/query_cost_map.py` maps multipliers correctly:
  - `Attribute.values` has `multipliers: ["limit"]`
  - `SelectedAttribute.values` does not
- A regression test fails on old behavior and passes with the fix.

files_to_modify.reason: Contains the COST_MAP entries for GraphQL query complexity. Currently has `SelectedAttribute.values` with `multipliers: ["limit"]` even though SelectedAttribute.values has no `limit` argument. Needs to move the multiplier to `Attribute.values` and remove it from SelectedAttribute.
Holds existing query cost validation tests. Needs new/updated tests to cover cost multiplication for `Attribute.values(limit: ...)` and ensure no multiplication for `SelectedAttribute.values`.

functions_to_modify.reason: Add `multipliers: ["limit"]` so the cost scales with the `limit` argument on `Attribute.values`.
Remove incorrect `multipliers: ["limit"]` because the SelectedAttribute.values field has no `limit` argument and is not paginated.

functions_to_modify.function_name: test(s) to add for Attribute.values cost multiplier
functions_to_modify.reason: Add regression coverage proving that `limit` affects cost for `Attribute.values` queries (and that the previous incorrect placement would not).

output.analysis_summary: Located the query cost configuration in `saleor/graphql/query_cost_map.py`. Confirmed the schema defines `Attribute.values(limit: Int = settings.NESTED_QUERY_LIMIT)` in `saleor/graphql/attribute/types.py`, while `SelectedAttribute.values` has no `limit` argument. The COST_MAP currently applies `multipliers: ["limit"]` to `SelectedAttribute.values`, which is inconsistent with the schema and matches the PR description. The fix should adjust COST_MAP accordingly and add a regression test in `saleor/graphql/core/tests/test_query_cost_validation.py`.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
Fix incorrect multipler for limit field

## PR Body
I want to merge this change because we recently introduced the `values` field with input limit. By accident the query cost was added to incorrect place. This PR fixes this. 

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> The changes on `SelectedAttribute` is expected. We don't have `limit` there, and it is not paginated.  (The query cost for this field will be handled separatly)

Impact checklist: no migrations/api changes/etc marked.

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
### MASCA (optimized for this PR)

#### M — Modify (what/where to change)
The bug is in the GraphQL query cost configuration for the newly introduced `Attribute.values(limit: Int)` field. The query-cost “multiplier” should apply to `Attribute.values` (because `limit` argument exists there), but it was mistakenly added under `SelectedAttribute.values` (which has no `limit` argument and is not paginated).

Primary change:
- Move/remove the `limit` multiplier from `SelectedAttribute.values` entry in the cost map.
- Ensure `Attribute.values` entry in the cost map uses `multipliers: ["limit"]`.

#### A — Affected behavior (expected result)
- Query cost should scale with `Attribute.values(limit: ...)`.
- `SelectedAttribute.values` should *not* use `limit` as multiplier (since the argument doesn’t exist there). Its cost should remain constant for now (as noted in the PR description).

#### S — Scope (blast radius)
- Only impacts query cost validation (complexity limiting) and does not change schema, resolvers, or DB.
- Can affect which queries are rejected/accepted under `GRAPHQL_QUERY_MAX_COMPLEXITY`.

#### C — Coverage (tests to add/adjust)
Add/adjust unit tests around `validate_query_cost` to assert:
- Cost increases when querying `Attribute.values(limit: N)`.
- No cost multiplier is applied for `SelectedAttribute.values`.

Most suitable place is the existing cost validator tests.

#### A — Acceptance criteria (done when)
- `saleor/graphql/query_cost_map.py` maps multipliers correctly:
  - `Attribute.values` has `multipliers: ["limit"]`
  - `SelectedAttribute.values` does not
- A regression test fails on old behavior and passes with the fix.


## Files to Modify
- saleor/graphql/query_cost_map.py: Contains the COST_MAP entries for GraphQL query complexity. Currently has `SelectedAttribute.values` with `multipliers: ["limit"]` even though SelectedAttribute.values has no `limit` argument. Needs to move the multiplier to `Attribute.values` and remove it from SelectedAttribute.
- saleor/graphql/core/tests/test_query_cost_validation.py: Holds existing query cost validation tests. Needs new/updated tests to cover cost multiplication for `Attribute.values(limit: ...)` and ensure no multiplication for `SelectedAttribute.values`.

## Functions to Modify
- COST_MAP (Attribute.values entry) (saleor/graphql/query_cost_map.py): Add `multipliers: ["limit"]` so the cost scales with the `limit` argument on `Attribute.values`.
- COST_MAP (SelectedAttribute.values entry) (saleor/graphql/query_cost_map.py): Remove incorrect `multipliers: ["limit"]` because the SelectedAttribute.values field has no `limit` argument and is not paginated.
- test(s) to add for Attribute.values cost multiplier (saleor/graphql/core/tests/test_query_cost_validation.py): Add regression coverage proving that `limit` affects cost for `Attribute.values` queries (and that the previous incorrect placement would not).

## Analysis Summary
Located the query cost configuration in `saleor/graphql/query_cost_map.py`. Confirmed the schema defines `Attribute.values(limit: Int = settings.NESTED_QUERY_LIMIT)` in `saleor/graphql/attribute/types.py`, while `SelectedAttribute.values` has no `limit` argument. The COST_MAP currently applies `multipliers: ["limit"]` to `SelectedAttribute.values`, which is inconsistent with the schema and matches the PR description. The fix should adjust COST_MAP accordingly and add a regression test in `saleor/graphql/core/tests/test_query_cost_validation.py`.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Move the query-cost multiplier to the correct GraphQL field by adding `multipliers: ["limit"]` to the `Attribute.values` COST_MAP entry (and ensuring its base cost remains unchanged).
steps.reason: `Attribute.values(limit: Int)` is the field that actually accepts the `limit` argument, so query complexity must scale with `limit` there; otherwise expensive queries can slip past the max complexity validation.
steps.side_effects: Queries using `Attribute.values(limit: N)` may be rejected more often under `GRAPHQL_QUERY_MAX_COMPLEXITY` because their computed cost will now correctly increase with `N`; no schema/resolver changes.

steps.operation: Remove the incorrect `multipliers: ["limit"]` configuration from the `SelectedAttribute.values` COST_MAP entry so its cost is constant for now.
steps.reason: `SelectedAttribute.values` does not define a `limit` argument and is not paginated, so applying a `limit` multiplier is both incorrect and potentially breaks cost calculation/validation assumptions for that field (and can also mislead future maintenance).
steps.side_effects: Queries using `SelectedAttribute.values` will no longer have any cost scaling based on a non-existent argument; overall complexity may decrease for those queries; future work may add a different cost model for this field as noted in the PR body.

steps.operation: Add/adjust query-cost validation tests to assert that `Attribute.values(limit: N)` increases computed query cost with `N`, and that `SelectedAttribute.values` does not apply a `limit` multiplier.
steps.function_to_modify: test(s) to add for Attribute.values cost multiplier
steps.reason: Provides regression coverage for the bug (multiplier placed on the wrong type) and locks in the intended behavior: only the field that accepts `limit` should be multiplied by it.
steps.side_effects: May require updating expected complexity numbers/snapshots if existing tests implicitly relied on the previous (incorrect) cost map; tests should be written to compare relative costs (e.g., limit=1 vs limit=10) to reduce brittleness.

step_plan.summary: Fix GraphQL query complexity calculation by applying the `limit` multiplier to `Attribute.values(limit: Int)` and removing the incorrect multiplier from `SelectedAttribute.values`. Add regression tests to ensure query cost scales with `limit` only where the argument exists.
